question: How is GitHub Copilot best described?

options.text: An AI-powered coding assistant ("AI pair programmer") that suggests and generates code and answers questions in your editor
A code formatter that enforces a fixed style guide
A CI/CD service that builds and deploys your repositories
A package manager for installing open-source dependencies

explanation: GitHub Copilot is an AI-powered coding assistant—an "AI pair programmer"—that helps you generate, understand, refactor, and debug code using inline suggestions and a chat experience.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / AI pair programmer

question: GitHub Copilot was developed in collaboration with which company and is originally powered by which system?

options.text: OpenAI, powered by the OpenAI Codex system
Google, powered by the Gemini system
Anthropic, powered by the Claude system
Meta, powered by the Llama system

explanation: Microsoft developed GitHub Copilot in collaboration with OpenAI, and it is powered by the OpenAI Codex system, which was trained on a large concentration of public source code.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / AI pair programmer

question: In which of the following environments is GitHub Copilot available as an extension? (Choose all that apply.)

options.text: Visual Studio Code
Visual Studio
Vim/Neovim
The JetBrains suite of IDEs
Microsoft Word

explanation: GitHub Copilot is available as an extension for VS Code, Visual Studio, Vim/Neovim, and the JetBrains suite of IDEs.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / AI pair programmer

question: What does the Copilot chat experience inside a supported editor let you do?

options.text: Ask questions about your code, get explanations of logic or errors, and generate tests or documentation using your project context
Only autocomplete a single line of code at a time with no conversation
Replace your editor's source control with a built-in Git client
Deploy your application to a cloud provider

explanation: Copilot chat is an interactive experience in supported editors. It understands your code context so you can ask questions, get explanations of logic or errors, and generate tests, documentation, or implementation ideas.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / AI pair programmer

question: When you open a pull request, how can GitHub Copilot help reviewers?

options.text: It can automatically generate a summary description of the changes
It automatically merges the pull request once checks pass
It deletes the source branch after review
It assigns reviewers based on repository billing

explanation: Copilot can automatically generate a pull request summary of the changes, helping reviewers understand intent and reducing the effort of writing PR descriptions.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / AI pair programmer

question: What is the purpose of the GitHub Copilot command-line interface (CLI)?

options.text: To ask for command suggestions, generate shell scripts, and understand command output or errors in the terminal
To replace Git as the version control system
To host static websites from the terminal
To manage organization billing from the command line

explanation: Copilot for the CLI helps in the terminal: you can ask for command suggestions and code snippets, generate shell scripts, and understand output or errors.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / AI pair programmer

question: How do inline suggestions appear, and how do you accept one in your editor?

options.text: They appear as grayed-out text ahead of the cursor, and you accept by pressing the Tab key
They appear in a popup dialog, and you accept by pressing Enter twice
They appear highlighted in red, and you accept by pressing Esc
They appear only in the chat panel, and you accept by clicking Submit

explanation: Inline suggestions show as grayed-out text ahead of your cursor. Press Tab (or the right-arrow key) to accept; keep typing or press Esc to reject.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Interact with Copilot

question: How do you reject an inline suggestion that Copilot offers?

options.text: Keep typing or press the Esc key
Press the Tab key
Press the right-arrow key
Save the file

explanation: To reject an inline suggestion, simply keep typing or press Esc. Tab and the right-arrow key accept the suggestion.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Interact with Copilot

question: What is inline chat in GitHub Copilot used for?

options.text: Context-specific conversations directly within the editor to request code changes or explanations without switching context
Chatting with other developers in your organization in real time
Posting questions to the public GitHub Community forum
Sending email summaries of your commits

explanation: Inline chat (opened with Ctrl+I / Cmd+I) enables context-specific conversations within the editor so you can request modifications or explanations for a specific code location without switching contexts.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Interact with Copilot

question: In Visual Studio Code, where can you find Copilot actions such as "Explain This" or "Generate Unit Tests"?

options.text: The command palette (Ctrl+Shift+P / Cmd+Shift+P), by typing "Copilot"
The repository Settings page on GitHub.com
The Git blame view
The organization billing dashboard

explanation: Open the command palette with Ctrl+Shift+P (Cmd+Shift+P on Mac), type "Copilot," and choose actions like "Explain This" or "Generate Unit Tests."
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Interact with Copilot

question: Which GitHub Copilot plan lets an individual developer use Copilot at no cost with limited monthly code completions and chat requests?

options.text: GitHub Copilot Free
GitHub Copilot Business
GitHub Copilot Enterprise
GitHub Copilot Pro+

explanation: GitHub Copilot Free lets individual developers use Copilot at no cost, including limited monthly completions and chat requests plus access to supported AI models.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Subscription plans

question: An organization wants centralized policy management, public-code filtering, and IP indemnity for its developers' Copilot usage. Which plan provides these?

options.text: GitHub Copilot Business
GitHub Copilot Free
GitHub Copilot Pro
GitHub Copilot Pro+

explanation: GitHub Copilot Business adds centralized management and policy controls, security vulnerability filtering, code referencing / public code filtering, and IP indemnity—features aimed at organizations.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Subscription plans

question: Which capability is unique to GitHub Copilot Enterprise (beyond what Business offers)?

options.text: Personalized code suggestions based on the organization's internal/private code by indexing its codebase
Inline code completions in the editor
Access to Copilot chat in the IDE
The ability to reject suggestions with the Esc key

explanation: Copilot Enterprise includes everything in Business plus personalization—it can index an organization's codebase to give tailored suggestions, AI-powered search/documentation, and deep GitHub integration.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Subscription plans

question: What distinguishes GitHub Copilot Pro+ from GitHub Copilot Pro?

options.text: Pro+ adds additional premium model request capacity and priority infrastructure access on top of all Pro features
Pro+ removes the ability to use Copilot chat
Pro+ is free while Pro is paid
Pro+ only works in the CLI, not in editors

explanation: Copilot Pro+ includes all Pro features plus additional premium request capacity and priority access to high-demand infrastructure, for individuals with higher usage needs.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Introduction to GitHub Copilot / Subscription plans

question: What are Microsoft and GitHub's six principles of Responsible AI?

options.text: Fairness; Reliability and safety; Privacy and security; Inclusiveness; Transparency; Accountability
Speed; Scalability; Security; Simplicity; Savings; Support
Accuracy; Automation; Availability; Auditing; Authentication; Authorization
Fairness; Profitability; Performance; Privacy; Portability; Predictability

explanation: The six principles are Fairness, Reliability and safety, Privacy and security, Inclusiveness, Transparency, and Accountability.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Responsible AI with GitHub Copilot / Six principles

question: Which Responsible AI principle states that AI systems should treat all people fairly and avoid differential impacts on similarly situated groups?

options.text: Fairness
Transparency
Accountability
Reliability and safety

explanation: Fairness means AI systems should treat everyone fairly, giving consistent recommendations to people in similar situations and using techniques to detect and mitigate bias.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Responsible AI with GitHub Copilot / Six principles

question: The Responsible AI principle that AI systems should be understandable maps to which principle?

options.text: Transparency
Inclusiveness
Privacy and security
Fairness

explanation: Transparency means AI systems should be understandable—people should be able to comprehend how decisions are made.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Responsible AI with GitHub Copilot / Six principles

question: Under the Responsible AI principles, who should be accountable for AI systems?

options.text: People should be accountable for AI systems
The AI model itself is solely accountable
Only the end users are accountable
No accountability is required for AI systems

explanation: Accountability means people—not the AI—are responsible for how AI systems operate and the outcomes they produce.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Responsible AI with GitHub Copilot / Six principles

question: Which Responsible AI principle focuses on AI systems performing consistently as intended and responding safely to unexpected conditions?

options.text: Reliability and safety
Inclusiveness
Transparency
Accountability

explanation: Reliability and safety means AI systems must operate reliably, safely, and consistently—functioning as designed, handling unexpected conditions safely, and resisting harmful manipulation.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Responsible AI with GitHub Copilot / Six principles

question: Which Responsible AI principle is concerned with AI systems empowering everyone and engaging people, regardless of ability or background?

options.text: Inclusiveness
Privacy and security
Reliability and safety
Accountability

explanation: Inclusiveness means AI systems should empower and engage everyone, avoiding the exclusion of any group of people.
source: Copilot Fundamentals · Responsible AI with GitHub Copilot / Six principles
